text: Nature Communications | Perspective | tutorial precomputed snapshot
CO2 hydrogenation to high-value products via heterogeneous catalysis

[DEMO PARAPHRASE] This Perspective reviews heterogeneous catalytic routes from carbon dioxide and renewable hydrogen to fuels and chemicals, with emphasis on methanol-mediated and Fischer-Tropsch-based routes.

[DEMO PARAPHRASE] The authors describe bifunctional systems that combine methanol synthesis with downstream dehydration or coupling, and they summarize previously published Cu- and In-based catalyst results.
